# MiOS-DEV/MiOS-bootstrap

> Interactive ignition installer for MiOS. User-facing entry point.
> Snapshot v0.2.0. The system layer (Containerfile, FHS overlay, build infra)
> lives in https://github.com/MiOS-DEV/MiOS.

MiOS is an immutable, bootc-native workstation OS on Fedora. This repository
contains only the bootstrap installer -- the script users run as root to
create their MiOS account, set hostname/password, install an SSH key, and
trigger the system install (either `bootc switch` for bootc hosts or a
clone-and-FHS-overlay for non-bootc Fedora).

## Files

- install.sh - Interactive installer (the user entry point)
- system-prompt.md - SSOT for AI behavior on every MiOS host (deployed to /etc/mios/ai/)
- README.md  - User-facing overview
- VARIABLES.md - Documentation of every prompt and default
- USER-SPACE-GUIDE.md - User-side environment notes
- .env.mios - User-runtime defaults
- identity.env.example - Identity template
- image-versions.yml - Pinned image references

## Install command

```bash
sudo bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/MiOS-DEV/MiOS-bootstrap/main/install.sh)"
```

## Defaults convention

Everything defaults to `mios` -- username, hostname, full name -- until the
user overrides at the prompt. This is intentional: a vanilla MiOS install
produces a host named `mios` with a sudoer named `mios`. The user only
types when they want something different.

## Standards alignment

- OpenAI REST API (open spec, multi-vendor)
- Linux Filesystem Hierarchy Standard (FHS 3.0)
- XDG Base Directory specification
- systemd / Quadlet
- bootc / OCI image format
